title: ranunculus bulbosus subsp. aleae
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. aleae, also known as Aleutian buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing swelling and pain, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots can be dried and used as a tea or tincture, or made into a salve or ointment for topical application.
